title: A Developer's Guide to Web Accessibility (WCAG)

questions.sentence: Web accessibility means ensuring that websites are usable by everyone including people with disabilities
To achieve this developers follow the Web Content Accessibility Guideline's WCAG
WCAG is based on four main principles Perceivable Operable Understandable and Robust
For example Perceivable means providing alternatives for nontext content like alt text for images
Operable means making all functionality available from a keyboard so users don't have to rely on a mouse
Many countries now have laws requiring websites to meet WCAG standards
